title: A Rooftop Clash Against Kaido and Big Mom
description: On pages 4–7, this part focuses on Unknown person #5, Unknown person #6, Unknown person #7, Kaido, Unknown person #8, King as The Worst Generation confronts Kaido and Big Mom on Onigashima.
scene: On the rooftop of Onigashima, the Worst Generation challenges Kaido and Big Mom directly, turning the raid into a clash against emperors.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Didn't need you lot Savin' me.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: feh!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Shoulda left me adrift! That's what you shoulda done...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: CASTAWAY RUIBEI

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Sigh -

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: C'mon, old man...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: How can you complain after getting personal nursing from Nami, huh?! I'm jealous, damn it!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Want me to go break something?! well?! cuz I'll do it!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: So if I'm getting this right...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: ...you're a chef from a nearby island.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And since your only daughter is getting married...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...you figured you'd get the legendary fish to serve at her wedding feast.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But on your way home after buying some, you got caught in a sudden squall...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...and hurt yourself trying to ride it out.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You're lucky we came along. Most people would be thanking us...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So what're you in such a sour mood for?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Give that back!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Wait a minute! Never mind that...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: The wedding feast is Tomor- Row?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: We have to get you home fast!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Ooh, did you hear?! legendary fish!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Yeah, I like the sound of that!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: I wonder how yummy they taste.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Hey! We'll take you back to your island...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...so let us have some of the fish, 'Kay?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Didn't you hear him? It's for a wedding feast!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If you want 'em, you can take 'em all.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Eat 'em. Toss 'em back in the sea. Do whatever you want.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Doom

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Wow, look at those fish!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: They're beautiful!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: NEAT! HOW MANY DO YOU THINK THERE ARE?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: This giant barrel is packed full!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Whoa, Whoa. Don't tell me...

dialogue.speaker: Kaido
dialogue.text: Are these Dragonic Super- Fillet Salmon?!

dialogue.speaker: Kaido
dialogue.text: DRACONIC SUPER-FILLET SALMON also called "Dragon Salmon."

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They're a type of salmon whose long, sinuous body brings to mind the imaginary dragon.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Only during their mating season do they gather, swimming in schools of hundreds.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Their flesh is considered far more tender and delicious than even beef tenderloin...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...and is thought to bring good fortune to those lucky enough to eat it.

dialogue.speaker: Kaido
dialogue.text: Yep. Dragon salmon.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But dressing and filtering them takes exacting skill.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: There isn't a soul on my island who could do it.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So I plunked down a lot of money to buy as many as I could to practice on, right?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: My friends laughed at me over it too.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Heh... looks like they were right!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Even if I hadn't gotten hurt, I never could've done it!

summary: This part advances the Wano / Rooftop Battle storyline through 47 dialogue beats, focusing on the raid’s momentum, character resolve, and the shifting battle lines.
